checks.a_analysis: The output content closely mirrors the source text without any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All text present in the JSON can be traced directly back to the source document, including the title, objectives, survey procedure, and schedule.
The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not introduce any fabricated contact information.
All sections from the single-page source document (Objectives, Secondary Objectives, Survey Procedure, Survey Schedule, and the footer note) are present in the output.
There are no tables in the source document, so this check is not applicable (PASS).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each section in the output contains the full text from the source. No significant sentences or key information have been omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the output is identical to the source document. Even typos in the original (e.g., 'magentics') are preserved faithfully. Formatting such as bolding and italics (e.g., for vessel names and software) has been maintained correctly.

checks.a_analysis: The output content follows the source document page flow exactly. Each paragraph, list, and heading appears once in the JSON, matching its single appearance in the source PDF. No duplicated blocks of text were identified.

The document title is correctly identified as the section title. While the sub-headers (Objectives, Survey Procedure, Survey Schedule) are rendered as bold/underlined text within paragraph elements rather than formal heading tags, this does not constitute a broken hierarchy (like skipping levels). The structural organization remains logical.
There are no tables present in the source document or the converted output.
The reading order in the JSON perfectly mirrors the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document, starting from the title and ending with the prepared-by footer.

The source document contains two lists: an ordered (numbered) list for secondary objectives and an unordered (bulleted) list for the schedule. The output correctly identifies the first list as 'ordered' and the second as 'unordered', maintaining all items and internal formatting (like bolding for days of the week).
No links are present in the source document, and none were injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output faithfully reflects the source PDF. Both documents utilize a single-column layout with consistent sequencing of headers, numbered lists, and bulleted lists. The logical flow of information is maintained.
There are no tables present in either the source PDF or the rendered HTML output.
The source PDF does not contain any meaningful images, charts, or logos. Consequently, no images were expected in the HTML output, and all textual content has been successfully captured.

No duplication of body content was observed. Although the document title appears at the very top of the HTML and again immediately below it, this is recognized as an intentional accessibility feature where a page heading (H1) precedes the actual document content. No paragraphs or list items are repeated.
